summary:
• Strait of Hormuz transit risks remain elevated despite peace deal optimism, with shipowners maintaining cautious positioning and major carriers like Lufthansa warning of significant cost impacts from potential closure scenarios.

• U.S. crude inventory draws are expected to continue due to record fuel exports and strong downstream demand, reducing domestic stockpile buffers at a time of heightened geopolitical supply route disruptions.

• Regional production resilience is emerging through joint Gulf of Mexico development projects and expanded downstream infrastructure in Australia, partially offsetting Western Hemisphere exposure to Middle East supply chain vulnerabilities.
